United's deployment of 10 737 MAXs to Guam signals confidence in MAX reliability while modernizing regional Pacific operations with improved fuel efficiency.

Military Mar 20, 2026

Italian Eurofighters Intercept Russian Su-30 That Violated Estonian Airspace Over the Baltic Sea

The Italian Air Force jets, deployed to Estonia to support NATO Baltic Air Policing mission, intercepted and escorted the Russian Navy Su-30SM. On Mar. 18, 2026, a Russian Navy Su-30SM multirole fighter entered Estonian airspace, which extends 12 NM (Nautical Miles) offshore from Estonia’s sovere...

NATO Baltic air policing operations demonstrate the critical role of forward-deployed fighter squadrons in deterring and responding to Russian airspace violations.

Commercial Mar 20, 2026

UK Aviation Explodes: 31 Flight Routes Hit 1 Million Passengers In 2025

The UK's three largest airports had 31 routes with 1 million+ passengers. But 2026 will bring a fourth airport to the list.

UK's major airport hubs are consolidating passenger volume on high-capacity routes, signaling market maturation and opportunities for network optimization.

See Simple Flying →

International Mar 20, 2026

How Russia Defied Sanctions To Maintain Its Long-Haul Commercial Fleet

In the wake of 2022 sanctions, Russia defied predictions of a grounded fleet by building a parallel, self-sustaining ecosystem to stay flying.

Russia's aircraft maintenance ecosystem demonstrates how geopolitical isolation can drive innovation in supply chain resilience, a lesson for global aviation operators facing future disruptions.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 20, 2026

NASA Simulations Improve Artemis II Launch Environment

Airflow around rockets as they travel from Earth into space can have a dramatic impact on a mission, which is why NASA used advanced simulations to provide the best possible launch conditions for the Artemis II test flight around the Moon. To better understand the Artemis Space Launch System (SLS...

Advanced airflow simulations optimize launch conditions for Artemis II, demonstrating how aerospace engineering precision ensures mission success.

NASA Glenn Opens Applications for Free Summer Engineering Institute

The hands-on learning experience is designed to help high school students prepare for a future in the aerospace workforce. Rising high school juniors and seniors can submit applic...

NASA's free summer engineering institute directly pipelines talented high school students into aerospace careers, addressing critical workforce shortages.

Aerospace Mar 20, 2026

Holistic space observation: the shift from SSA to SDA

Recent reporting on SpaceX’s proposal to deploy up to one million satellites in low Earth orbit — paired with a vision of AI-enabled, autonomous orbital infrastructure — marks a decisive moment for the space community. Regardless of whether these numbers ultimately materialize, the direction is u...

Space situational awareness is evolving into space domain awareness with AI-enabled autonomous systems, fundamentally changing orbital infrastructure management and aviation airspace coordination.

Military Mar 20, 2026

The US Air Force Won't Buy Any More KC-46s Until Issues Are Fixed

On March 4th, the US Air Force’s new Vice Chief of Staff told lawmakers that his branch won’t finalize a new deal with Boeing for another 75 KC-46 tankers until some of the “deficiencies” with the refueler are resolved. As you may or may not already know, the 767-based tanker transport has experi...

Boeing's KC-46 tanker program faces a critical halt on new orders until quality issues are resolved, signaling stricter Air Force procurement standards.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 20, 2026

ESA to fly dedicated Crew Dragon mission to ISS

The European Space Agency plans to charter a SpaceX Crew Dragon mission to the International Space Station to give more flight opportunities for its astronauts. The post ESA to fly dedicated Crew Dragon mission to ISS appeared first on SpaceNews.

ESA's charter of dedicated Crew Dragon flights ensures reliable ISS access for European astronauts independent of traditional partnership arrangements.

See SpaceNews →

Industry Mar 20, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Optimizing airline, airport, and flight operations through advanced technologies is critical to managing growth while reducing aviation's environmental impact.

See MDPI →

International Mar 20, 2026

How Iran Secretly Built a Boeing Fleet Under Sanctions

For decades, Iran has operated one of the most unusual airline fleets in the world. Despite sweeping international sanctions designed to isolate the country from the global aerospace industry, Iranian airlines still fly dozens of Boeing and Airbus aircraft. Many of those jets were never supposed ...

Iran's sanctions-evasion aircraft procurement through shell companies and secondary markets demonstrates critical vulnerabilities in aviation supply chain compliance monitoring.

See Simple Flying →

SWISS's A350 first-class suites represent a premium differentiation strategy that justifies higher fares through enclosed privacy and bespoke amenities.

Kayhan Space is branching out from providing orbital intelligence used to coordinate satellite fleets with a new software platform that turns that data into business insights for investors and insurers. The post Kayhan targets investors, insurers with expanded orbital intelligence platform appear...

Kayhan's orbital intelligence platform expansion into investor and insurer markets creates new revenue streams by monetizing satellite data analytics beyond fleet coordination.

Why Doesn't The World's Fastest Commercial Aircraft Have Winglets?

The Boeing 747-8's innovative raked wingtips offer a sleek, integrated solution to reduce induced drag.

The 747-8's raked wingtips achieve drag reduction through integrated design rather than traditional winglets, optimizing aerodynamic efficiency.

DJI’s smallest drone just hit it’s lowest price… again

DJI’s smallest drone has just gotten cheaper over on Amazon. The DJI Neo, DJI’s cheapest drone, is for a short time having a $50 coupon code available on Amazon, bringing the price down to just $150. This follows an earlier price drop last month. more…

DJI's aggressive pricing strategy for consumer drones continues to compress entry-level market segments, signaling intensifying competition in accessible drone technology.

DJI Mini 4K now priced like a budget accessory

DJI's aggressive pricing on consumer drones continues to democratize aerial imagery, expanding the market of potential commercial drone operators.

Russian Aerospace Forces publicized for the first time the MiG-31 armed with the Kh-47M2 Kinzhal aeroballistic hypersonic missile flying in the Indo-Pacific region. Russia’s Ministry of Defense (RuMoD) released footage on Mar. 17, 2026, of its MiG-31I jets carrying the Kh-47M2 Kinzhal hypersonic ...

Russia's deployment of Kinzhal-armed MiG-31s in the Indo-Pacific signals escalating hypersonic missile capabilities in strategically contested airspace.
